column2:
Wolves
(Collabaration with Kanye West)
(New Abu Dhabi, Calabasas Is The New Abu Dhabi)
column3:
(01/??/2016) (Kanye & Consequence travel to Drake's house for collab album sessions)
(??/??/2018) (Album never officially releases, according to Consequence, due to Kanye West & Kid Cudi's album "KIDS SEE GHOSTS")
column6: Wolves was meant to be a collabaration album between Kanye West & Drake. The project was first mentioned by Ye in an interview in 2015, and billboards would be spotted with the phrase "Calabasas Is The New Abu Dhabi" with the OVO and G.O.O.D. Music logos in mid-2016 which hinted the album was coming soon. Drake later even stated the album was finished and up to Kanye to release, but despite this multiple insiders have stated that Wolves was mainly just a session and nothing much came out of it. The cover used for this era is a recreation of the image used on the billboards.

column3:
OG Filename: FRIENDFLIP 77
Beat meant for the Wolves sessions. It is unknown if Drake or Kanye actually recorded on it though. Leaked as a bonus for the 38 minute version of the VULTURES 2 throwaway "THIRSTY" on 12/15/24. Used by J. Cole for his album "The Fall Off" near enough 10 years later.
The original version of Glow. There are no Drake or Kanye verses on this song, only PND.

(I Hope)
column3: CyHi confirmed early unfinished versions of "Glow" existed. He said Kanye "played it for [him]...but Kanye kinda already knew where he wanted to go with it. I just helped them, I'm just in there like, 'I like the way you said that, or I think you should go up on that'". Highly likely he also recorded for it.

A demo Kanye recorded for in the same vocal session as "Pop Style." Recorded in late January 2016. Lossless version leaked after its instrumental + acapella were found in leaked "30 Hours" session files.
The filename of the leaked version implies a version of the beat exists with an 808

Version of "Wolves" confirmed by a trusted source. Could possibly have Drake doing a reference verse similar to Kanye's second verse on the final version. Most likely recorded in the "Wolves" sessions due to Kanye stating the song would be on the album. Confirmed to not feature Björk.

In a January 2023 interview, Consequence stated he recorded at a session at Drake's house for Wolves. Likely was just reference tracks.
According to trusted sources, a lot was done in the 2 week session at Drake's house, it's unknown what however.

column2:
🏆 Kanye West - 30 Hours [V6]
(ref. Drake) (prod. Karriem Riggins)
column3: Drake reference track, from when Kanye was recording his part. Can be heard in the bleed on an early Kanye vocal stem. Made in October 2015. Mentioned by Drake in his 2018 Pusha T diss track "Duppy Freestyle". Unsure if anyone owns this in CDQ and if it's just Drake doing the 2nd verse. Privately sold to members of the community during a unsuccessful groupbuy. Version linked is enhanced slightly to be able to hear Drake.

column3: Drake reference track. Mentioned by Drake in his 2018 Pusha T diss track "Duppy Freestyle". He has a writing credit on the released version, but it is unknown what he contributed. Possibly from the "Wolves" sessions.
